You could prob have an LLM make something like this for you pretty painlessly. The magic word you're missing is "d3", the javascript library that underlies most interactive data visualization tools.

posts.text: To see the code that generated that example: hover your mouse over the chart, this should reveal a "..." button to the left of the chart. Then click "edit" on that menu to reveal the code. Or just point an LLM at the URL and have it deal with it.

posts.text: Without accounting for network topology, you can't reason about communication rate, which is why the mean/median alternatives the authors consider here seem appropriate. reply_context.text: Aggregation rules need to account for network topology. The aggregation rules here presume something like a flat linear topology (where everyone is connected to a single vote tallier) or a fully connected clique (where everyone is mutually connected and can communicate instantaneously). 3/
